skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Helps draft internal communications such as status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, and project updates.
skill.summary: Draft structured internal communications for common company update formats.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography, and shell backtick execution, but review found these are false positives. The URLs and flagged wording are Apache License text, and the backticks in SKILL.md are Markdown filename formatting rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links. No network request, data transfer, or executable behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside LICENSE.txt and contain only Apache License reference URLs. There is no code path that could perform outbound network access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in Apache License prose and not in cryptographic implementation code. No hashing, encryption, or signature logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are legal boilerplate in a license file. They do not define or call any cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Around Filenames
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are inline Markdown code spans naming guideline files. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks appear in documentation bullets around paths such as examples/3p-updates.md. There is no script block, interpreter directive, or command execution instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Skill Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts in SKILL.md occur in descriptive text and fallback instructions. No cryptographic operation is described or implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only natural-language instructions for writing communications. The flagged lines do not include crypto APIs, algorithms, or executable code.

content.value_statement: Teams often need clear updates in familiar internal formats. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft status reports, newsletters, FAQs, and leadership updates.
actual_capabilities: Identifies common internal communication types from a user request.
Supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
Directs the assistant to use communication-specific formatting and tone guidance.
Asks for clarification when the requested format is unclear.
Provides keywords for routing internal communication requests.
limitations: The referenced examples directory is not included in this package.
It does not send messages or integrate with email, chat, or document systems.
It depends on the user to provide accurate facts, audience, and approval context.
It does not verify incident, project, or business data independently.

use_cases.title: Prepare Weekly Team Updates
use_cases.description: Turn project notes into a structured progress, plans, and problems update for recurring team communication.

use_cases.title: Draft Leadership Briefings
use_cases.description: Create concise updates that emphasize decisions, risks, status, and requested executive action.

use_cases.title: Answer Internal Questions
use_cases.description: Convert repeated employee questions into clear FAQ responses with consistent tone and scope.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Status Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a status report for this project. Audience: [team or leader]. Current progress: [facts]. Plans: [next steps]. Problems: [risks or blockers].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Company Newsletter Item
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for broad internal communication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a company newsletter section about [topic]. Include why it matters, what changed, who is affected, and what employees should do next.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Leadership Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a leadership update about [initiative]. Include current status, key metrics, decisions needed, risks, and the owner for each next step.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Incident Communication
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this incident update for internal stakeholders. Keep it factual, calm, and action-oriented. Clarify impact, timeline, mitigation, owners, and follow-up commitments.

output_examples.input: Draft a 3P update for the onboarding redesign project.
output_examples.output: Progress: The design review is complete and pilot feedback is summarized. Plans: The team will finalize copy and run a second pilot next week. Problems: Analytics tagging is delayed and needs an owner today.

output_examples.input: Write an FAQ answer about the new travel approval process.
output_examples.output: Employees should submit travel requests before booking. The approval flow checks budget, policy fit, and manager approval. Urgent travel can still be escalated through the normal exception path.

output_examples.input: Create a leadership update for a delayed launch.
output_examples.output: The launch is delayed by one week because final compliance review is still open. Customer impact remains low. The decision needed is whether to keep the announcement date or move all external messaging.

best_practices: Provide audience, communication type, deadline, and approval owner before drafting.
Separate confirmed facts from assumptions, risks, and open questions.
Review sensitive updates with legal, security, or leadership owners before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to invent project status, metrics, or incident facts.
Do not publish sensitive employee, customer, or security details without review.
Do not rely on the missing example files unless they are added to the package.

faq.question: What types of internal communication does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Does this skill include complete templates?
faq.answer: The skill references example guideline files, but those files are not included in the scanned package.

faq.question: Can it send messages for me?
faq.answer: No. It only helps draft content. Sending and approval steps must happen in your normal communication tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to use for sensitive incidents?
faq.answer: Use it for drafting only. Sensitive incident updates should be reviewed by the proper security, legal, or leadership owners.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, purpose, confirmed facts, desired format, timing, and any topics that must be avoided.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
